steps.title: Step 1 — Identify the task
steps.content: Words-in-Context. The colon signals that the clause after it explains the blank: "nearly a dozen of the characters that populate his novels are poets or writers." That evidence shows being a poet is COMMON in Delany's work.

steps.title: Step 2 — Choose the word
steps.content: "Atypical" means unusual. Saying being a poet is "not atypical" means it IS typical/common — which matches the evidence that nearly a dozen of his characters are poets or writers.

steps.title: Step 3 — Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘infallible’: means incapable of error; the evidence about frequency of poet characters has nothing to do with fallibility. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘lucrative’: means profitable; the supporting evidence makes no claim about poets' income. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘tedious’: means boring; the evidence about how often poet characters appear doesn't address tedium. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
